│ #  │ Skill Name              │ Times │ Last Context                  │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ 1  │ openspec-new-change     │   1   │ User asked to start a new     │
│    │                         │       │ change                        │
│ 2  │ openspec-skill-audit    │   1   │ Current audit request         │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Details:
  [1] openspec-new-change
      → Context: "User asked to start a new change"
      → Status: ✓ Loaded successfully

  [2] openspec-skill-audit
      → Context: "User asked to verify whether openspec-new-change was triggered"
      → Status: ✓ Loaded successfully (this run)

---

PowerShell terminal output commands:

Write-Host "`n╔══════════════════════════════════════════════╗" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "║           SKILL INVOCATION AUDIT             ║" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════╝`n" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "  Session summary: 2 skill(s) invoked, 2 unique skill(s)`n" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host "  ✓ openspec-new-change" -ForegroundColor Green -NoNewline
Write-Host "  (1x) — User asked to start a new change" -ForegroundColor Gray
Write-Host "  ✓ openspec-skill-audit" -ForegroundColor Green -NoNewline
Write-Host "  (1x) — User asked to verify whether openspec-new-change was triggered" -ForegroundColor Gray
Write-Host "`n✓ Skills are triggering correctly." -ForegroundColor Green

---

Observation: ✓ Skills are triggering correctly.

openspec-new-change appears in the conversation history as a skill tool invocation.
It was triggered when the user asked to start a new change, and loaded successfully.
